title: Wholesome Thai Pork Salad
description: Floral, crisp Thai pork salad that doubles as a hangover cure, featuring garlicky lardons and bright herbs.
instructions: Slice the pork tenderloin into thin strips and season with salt and pepper.
Cut the smoked bacon into small lardons and render them in a skillet until crisp, then set aside.
Heat a nonstick pan to medium-high, add the pork strips and sear until cooked through to 63C internal temperature, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the rendered bacon and a splash of fish sauce to the pan, cooking for an additional 1 minute.
In a small bowl whisk together lime juice, soy sauce, palm sugar, and chili flakes until sugar dissolves.
Toss the shredded cabbage, sliced cucumber, cilantro, and mint in a large bowl, then add the pork and bacon mixture.
Pour the dressing over the salad, toss until well coated, then sprinkle crushed peanuts on top.
Serve immediately, garnished with extra mint if desired.
